# DSLRaid Backend Targets

<!-- generated by scripts/backendgen.sh; do not edit by hand -->

Backends consume Canonical IR or projection products.
Common Lisp forms remain the native authoring SSOT.

| Target | Role | Input | Lossy | Contract |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| rust | generated runtime source | Canonical IR | no | Rust can be runtime code or generated output; Lisp forms stay SSOT. |
| go | generated runtime source | Canonical IR | no | Go output follows the same modeled FSM behavior contract. |
| typescript | viewer/runtime adapter source | Canonical IR | no | TypeScript output is derived tooling surface, not authoring truth. |
| mermaid | documentation diagram | Canonical IR | yes | Mermaid export is for readable docs and may omit executable detail. |
| dot | dense graph layout input | Canonical IR | yes | DOT export is for layout/debugging and may omit architecture metadata. |
| svg | static visualization | ViewModel | yes | SVG renders a projection snapshot, not the full IR. |
| markdown | generated documentation | Canonical IR | partial | Docs explain derived structure while preserving traceable subjects. |
